How Tom Brady built a $5 million car collection after starting with a $200 Dodge Dart



Tom Brady didn't just collect Super Bowl rings during his NFL career; he built one of the more loaded garages in sports too. From a $3 million hypercar to a custom Aston Martin with his own initials stamped on it, here's a clean breakdown of what he's actually got, what it's worth, and how it all started.What stands out most isn't just the price tags, it's the range. The Bugatti and the Aston Martins are pure flex territory, the kind of cars that exist mainly to be rare and fast. The Rolls-Royce is the practical family pick, built for comfort over speed. And the Audi R8, while still a six-figure car, is essentially his "everyday" supercar by comparison to the rest of the garage.The most interesting part of the whole collection might actually be the cheapest car on the list. Long before the Super Bowl rings and Aston Martin endorsement deals, Brady's very first car was a $200 1967 Dodge Dart that he bought off his own sister. He didn't upgrade to anything flashy until he made it big in the league, when he picked up a 2004 Cadillac Escalade as his first real splurge. Everything else in the garage came after that, once the wins, the endorsements, and the money started piling up.One caveat worth flagging here: total car count and overall value swing depending on the outlet, and none of these numbers come directly from Brady or his team, so the totals should be treated as estimates rather than confirmed figures. The individual car details- the Bugatti, the Aston Martins, the Rolls Royce, the Audi, the Dodge Dart, are consistent across multiple sources and solid to run with.
Article Summary: Tom Brady's $5 million car collection includes a Bugatti, Aston Martins, Rolls Royce, the Audi, and a Dodge Dart. Brady's first car was a $200 1967 Dodge Dart that he bought off his own sister. He didn't upgrade to anything flashy until he made it big in the league.

Article Title: Michael Pennel Jr. had a secret relationship with the woman whose body was found buried on his property, witnesses say
Article Text:
Image - X

NFL champ Mike Pennel had a relationship with a woman found dead on his property — his own lawyer expects charges



Where things stand now



"I don't know this woman. I wasn't in the country"

Mike Pennel Jr., the former Kansas City Chiefs defensive lineman and two-time Super Bowl champion, is at the center of a homicide investigation in the Dominican Republic after a new ESPN report found that witnesses told police in 2021 he had an ongoing relationship with a woman whose body was later found on land he owned. The report directly contradicts Pennel's repeated denials, and even his own attorney now says he expects Pennel to be charged.The woman has been identified as Carli Franchesca Guzmán Roche, 22, who was reported missing by her family on September 11, 2021, after moving to Sosúa in the Dominican Republic. Her remains weren't discovered until January 2026, when a new owner doing excavation work on the property, which Pennel owned at the time and has since sold, came across them. Her grandmother, Paula González, told ESPN her granddaughter regularly referred to Pennel as "the football player" and that she'd seen the two of them on video calls together. Shown a photo of Pennel, she said there was "no doubt" he was the man her granddaughter was seeing.Records reviewed by ESPN show people told police as early as 2021 that Pennel's connection to Guzmán was worth investigating, and that investigators visited his property with her family days after she vanished, where a strong smell of decay was reportedly noticed but written off as a dead animal.Police apparently never circled back to question Pennel himself. Adding to the confusion, Pennel now claims it was actually his friend who had a relationship with Guzmán, not him, even though that same friend told police back in 2021 that he'd seen Pennel and Guzmán leave the airport together in a taxi.Pennel has been firm in denying any wrongdoing, telling ESPN,when she disappeared. But his Dominican attorney, Ariel Durán, told ESPN something striking: he expects Pennel to actually be charged in the case simply because the body was found on his property, and said he may not continue representing Pennel if that happens due to an unrelated dispute.As of now, a Dominican court document confirms no criminal charges have been filed. Dominican officials reopened the case after identifying Guzmán's remains and are investigating it as a homicide, with Pennel named a person of interest, though the cause of death hasn't been released. Pennel, now a free agent, says he's shared the no-charges document with the NFL.
Article Summary: Mike Pennel Jr. is at the center of a homicide investigation in the Dominican Republic after a new ESPN report found that witnesses told police in 2021 he had an ongoing relationship with a woman whose body was later found on his property. The report directly contradicts Pennel's repeated denials, and even his own attorney now says he expects Pennel to be charged.

Article Title: Alix Earle moved on from Tom Brady fast and straight into Benson Boone's arms
Article Text:
Tom Brady and Alix Earle (Getty Images)

Alix Earle's rockstar dream comes true as she steps out with Benson Boone



Is Alix Earle finally moving on? Tom Brady came and went, now there's Benson Boone



Alix Earle has had a wild six months on the dating front, and it looks like she might finally be landing somewhere good. After a tearful breakup from NFL star Braxton Berrios in December, a very public flirtation with Tom Brady, and months of visible ups and downs in between, she was just spotted having dinner with singer Benson Boone, and this one might actually mean something.She said in a YouTube video in April that dating a rock star was "literally my dream," and weeks later she went viral lip-syncing one of Benson Boone's unreleased songs on a yacht.Boone recreated the clip himself and tagged her. Then came the real-life version: this week, Earle and Boone were seen leaving Craig's restaurant in West Hollywood together, with Boone walking her to his truck and opening the passenger door for her.When TMZ asked Boone point-blank if they were dating, he said "Uh, no," calling it "just hanging out." But the timing is hard to read as nothing. Earle is set to star in Boone's new music video, "The Time of My Life," dropping June 26, and the two have been spending real time together off-camera tooEarle and Berrios split in December after roughly two years together, and she didn't try to hide how hard it hit her. She posted a teary TikTok explaining the breakup came down to long distance and the two of them being on "different paths," carefully noting that neither of them had done anything wrong.The months that followed played out almost entirely in public. There was a late-night crying video where she talked to the camera "like you're my boyfriend," and posts in February about learning that being sad doesn't mean the breakup was the wrong call.Then came Tom Brady. Earle rang in the New Year partying with him on a yacht in St. Barths, and the two were spotted together again in Vegas in March at The Wynn.Nothing about that ever turned into anything official. Still, it was the first real sign she was stepping back into the dating pool, and it set off months of speculation about whether something serious was actually brewing there.That speculation never fully landed anywhere, and by spring, Earle had shifted her focus.So where does that leave Tom Brady in all this? Probably exactly where it always was, a fun, high-profile rebound moment that never had real legs, rather than the start of anything lasting. Boone, on the other hand, checks every box Earle said she wanted: young, a musician, and someone she's already built a real connection with through work. Whether or not he ever confirms it, this is the first time since Berrios that Earle looks like she's actually moving forward, not just moving on.
Article Summary: Alix Earle and Benson Boone were spotted together in West Hollywood. Boone said they were "just hanging out," but the timing is hard to read as nothing. Earle is set to star in Boone's new music video, "The Time of My Life," dropping June 26.

Article Title: Madison Beer just threw shade at all her exes while gushing over Justin Herbert
Article Text:
Justin Herbert and Madison Beer (Image via X: Justin Herbert and Madison Beer)

Madison Beer says Justin Herbert is the first guy who's ever made her forget the world exists



Why does this one stand out from her past relationships



Madison Beer just said something about Justin Herbert that's bigger than it sounds. On a podcast this week, she described a feeling she says she's never had before, total tunnel vision, just from talking to her boyfriend. Coming from someone who's been brutally honest about her dating history on multiple podcasts over the years, this isn't just a throwaway line for the cameras.Beer told Shane that when she's deep in conversation with Herbert, she loses all sense of everything else happening around her. Her hair could be on fire, she said, and she wouldn't notice. The world could be ending around her and she wouldn't realize it. She called it the first time in her life she's ever felt that way about someone. She also revealed that their actual first date happened at her house, not in public, because the two of them were too nervous to be seen together so early on.That tracks with what fans already knew: Beer and Herbert spent months quietly dodging cameras and dodging questions before they finally went Instagram-official back in October, kissing on the SoFi Stadium sidelines before a Chargers game.Here's why this comment lands differently than the usual new-relationship gushing celebrities do in interviews.Beer doesn't typically talk about her exes lightly, and she's rarely this direct about comparing a current partner to her past. Her last serious relationship, with social media personality Nick Austin, was on-and-off for years and ended messily in late 2024, and a meaningful chunk of her new album "Locket" was written right in the middle of that breakup, including the single "Bittersweet."She's been open in the past about feeling like she doesn't "know how to be alone," about a pattern of serial dating she's tried to break, and about deep-rooted abandonment issues she discussed at length with Alex Cooper on "Call Her Daddy." So when she says Herbert is the first person to ever give her this kind of calm, unshakeable focus, it reads less like a typical honeymoon-phase soundbite and more like a real, pointed contrast to a pattern she's been actively trying to move past. Whether that holds up long-term is anyone's guess. Right now, though, she's drawing a clear, deliberate line between this relationship and everything that came before it.
Article Summary: Madison Beer says Justin Herbert is the first guy who's ever made her forget the world exists. On a podcast this week, she described a feeling she says she's never had before. She also revealed that their actual first date happened at her house, not in public.

Article Title: Miami Dolphins CB Darrell Baker Jr gets trolled for saying ‘everybody thinks we’re rich…’ when he was making $22000 a month for practice
Article Text:
Miami Dolphins CB Darrell Baker Jr gets trolled for saying ‘everybody thinks we’re rich…’ when he was making $22000 a month for practice (Image via Getty: Darrell Baker Jr.)

Dolphins cornerback Darrell Baker Jr trashed for earning $22K per month

$22000 per week is more than decent pay and sounds rich

Miami Dolphins cornerback Darrell Baker Jr. revealed that he made only $5,500 per week on an NFL practice squad.He went on to say, “Everybody thinks we’re rich…”This comment did not sit well with an X user called “Peckins.”The user tagged My Mixtapez’s post on Baker and captioned it,“B*tch you making 22k a month to practice.”“You know how these NFL contracts work, coz everybody thinks we’re rich. Let’s get into it. Let’s talk about,” Darrell Baker Jr. said.“First year, my base salary was about $150,000.Coming in as a rookie, thinking I’m about to make $750K this year, and I got hit with reality.I didn’t make the $53K off the rip coming out of camp, so I was on the practice squad.When you’re on the practice squad, you don’t make the base salary of whatever’s on your contract.You get the minimum of what is set for the league. At that time, it was about $211,000.Now, you’re probably saying, ‘That’s still great money! What is he talking about?’But you only get paid during the season. On the practice squad, you don’t really know if you’re gonna be with that team the whole year.So, you can easily be released. With that, you have to pay moving costs, and you might have to break a lease, which is usually about 2 months of rent upfront.So, my first paycheck, and they took about 55 percent of my first check.You know, on the practice squad, I was making about $5,500 every week during the regular season.It’s still great money. But we’re definitely not rich.”After this video declaration came to light, several cybercitizens spoke out about it.An X account called FAUX MONIKER shared a screenshot of an alleged ESPN article showing how much NFL practice squad members were paid.The answer was: “Practice squad members are separated by salary into two groups --players with two years of service or less and players with two or more years of service.2025 totals: players with two years of service or less will receive $13,000 per week.And players with two or more years of service will receive a $17,500 minimum weekly salary [$22,000 maximum].”A commenter quickly commented on the ESPN article screenshot, saying,“Darrell Baker Jr. is NASTY for saying “they think we’re rich”. $22k per WEEK is MORE than decent pay!!!!”Another said, “Sounds rich to me.” A post read, “22k RIGHT NOW just one week would take me out of debt lmfao.”Darrell Baker Jr. signed a one-year, $1.4M contract with the Miami Dolphins in March 2026.The deal includes a $1.22M salary, a $1.08M in guaranteed money, and a $187,500 signing bonus. He carries a $1.26M cap hit for 2026.
Article Summary: Miami Dolphins cornerback Darrell Baker Jr. revealed that he made only $5,500 per week on an NFL practice squad. He went on to say, “Everybody thinks we’re rich…” This comment did not sit well with an X user called “Peckins.”

Article Title: Antonio Brown car collection: Inside $2 million worth car garage screaming Mercedes Benz, Ferrari, Rolls Royce, McLaren, and more
Article Text:
Antonio Brown car collection: Inside $2 million worth car garage screaming Mercedes Benz, Ferrari, Rolls Royce, McLaren, and more(Image via Getty: Antonio Brown)

Ex Patriots WR Antonio Brown’s lavish cars include Chevrolet , Mercedes Benz, and Ferrari

Custom Rolls-Royce Phantom

McLaren 720S Spider

Mercedes-AMG G 63

Royalty could be the middle name of Antonio Brown.When he spent 11 days with the New England Patriots,The wide receiver ordered $5,000 worth of nutrition-specific milk for his quarterback, Tom Brady, as revealed by Bill Belichick.The wide receiver radiates royalty through and through.No wonder his car collection is nothing short of a variety of Mercedes-Benz, Ferrari, Rolls-Royce, and McLaren.One of them even honors his Super Bowl-winning team, the Pittsburgh Steelers, in a special and unique way.Let’s find out more about his wheel collection, or should we say obsession? More like fascination…One of the most expensive cars.It matches the Pittsburgh Steelers’ team colors. He has shared several pictures of it on social media.The car is estimated at $500,000, but it could have cost Antonio Brown more due to the two custom paint schemes.The vehicle is powered by 6.75L twin-turbocharged V12 engine with an eight-speed automatic transmission.It produces 563 horsepower and 664 pound-foot of torque. It goes from 0 to 100 mph in 5.4 seconds.The former New England Patriots wide receiver has posted a picture of the posh vehicle on Instagram before.The cost of the car is estimated to be $315,000.It boasts a unique 4.0L twin-turbocharged V8 engine with a seven-speed dual-clutch automatic transmission.The engine has 710 horsepower and goes from 0 to 60 mph in 2.7 seconds.The vehicle’s top speed is 212 mph.The white luxury car features a rugged exterior and forceful core.The vehicle offers the comfort of a sedan and the off-roading features of an SUV.The off-roader boasts a 4L twin-turbocharged V8 engine with a nine-speed automatic transmission.The engine delivers 577 horsepower and 627 pound-foot of torque.Brown has been photographed driving this $315,000 car several times around his several homes.Only 799 Berlinetta editions were put on the market, making it the rarest of rare finds.It boasts a 6.3L V12 engine producing 731 horsepower.It goes from 0 to 60 mph in 3.1 seconds.This vehicle stands out in Brown’s collection of luxury cars because of its vintage look.The rest of his cars have an ultra-modern touch.It boasts 24-to 26-inch custom alloy wheels, and the roof features a burgundy theme.It features a massive V8 engine, and its top speed ranges from 155 to 245 mph.It could have been a convertible, costing around $30,000 before all the modifications.The total worth of Antonio Brown’s car collection is estimated to be $2 million.
Article Summary: Antonio Brown's car collection is estimated to be $2 million. His cars include Mercedes Benz, Ferrari, Rolls Royce, McLaren, and more. One of them even honors his Super Bowl-winning team, the Pittsburgh Steelers, in a special way.

DeMar DeRozan turns 37 before next season starts, and the Lakers are running out of time to figure out how many years LeBron James has left. That math is pushing Los Angeles toward a different kind of buy: not youth, not upside, just a proven scorer who shows up every night. A new mock trade floated by Sports Illustrated's Gray Deyo would send DeRozan from Sacramento to L.A. without touching James or Doncic.Sports Illustrated's Gray Deyo proposed a mock trade sending DeMar DeRozan and Drew Eubanks from the Sacramento Kings to the Los Angeles Lakers for Dalton Knecht, Jake LaRavia, Rui Hachimura and a top-4 protected 2030 first-round pick. DeRozan, 36, is averaging 19.2 points on 50.6% shooting this season and is owed roughly $25.7 million next year, with $10 million guaranteed. The deal would not require moving LeBron James or Luka Doncic.DeRozan isn't declining in any way that shows up on the stat sheet. He played 77 games this season, his fifth straight year clearing 70 appearances, and he's never dipped below 60 in a season outside his rookie year.His 18.4 points per game last season was a 13-year low for him, and it still would have led most NBA benches. Deyo's pitch leans on that reliability: an efficient scorer with seven years of playoff experience who thrives in exactly the slow, half-court possessions the playoffs produce.Pairing him with Doncic gives the Lakers a second read in crunch time without disturbing the James-Doncic core.The proposed deal centers on a straightforward premise. Los Angeles would add a proven scorer capable of operating in half-court settings, an area that often becomes decisive during playoff basketball.According to Sports Illustrated's Gray Deyo, DeRozan's experience and offensive efficiency make him an intriguing fit next to Doncic. The veteran forward has appeared in at least 70 games in each of the past five seasons and played 77 games for Sacramento last season. Durability remains one of his strongest selling points.For Los Angeles, the acquisition would signal an all-in approach around Doncic and James.DeRozan thrives in isolation and mid-range situations, areas that become increasingly valuable during postseason play. Eubanks would also fill a depth need in the frontcourt, providing another rotational center option.The challenge would be balancing touches among Doncic, James, Austin Reaves and DeRozan.Sacramento's benefit comes through long-term flexibility.DeRozan is not viewed as a foundational piece for the franchise's future, making this type of deal attractive if the organization chooses a different direction. Knecht and LaRavia are both 24 years old and could receive expanded opportunities in larger roles.Adding a future first-round pick would further strengthen the Kings' rebuilding assets.DeRozan is in the middle of a three-year contract worth approximately $74 million. He is owed around $25.7 million next season, though only $10 million is initially guaranteed.That structure makes him one of the more movable veteran contracts available this offseason and helps explain why his name continues to surface in trade discussions.DeRozan remains productive despite being one of the league's oldest high-usage wings.The Lakers have not completed any deal, and this remains a hypothetical trade scenario. Still, as Los Angeles searches for veteran help around Luka Doncic, DeRozan stands out as one of the most realistic high-profile options available this summer.
Article Summary: Sports Illustrated's Gray Deyo proposed a mock trade sending DeMar DeRozan and Drew Eubanks to the Los Angeles Lakers for Dalton Knecht, Jake LaRavia, Rui Hachimura and a top-4 protected 2030 first-round pick. The deal would not require moving LeBron James or Luka Doncic. The veteran forward has appeared in at least 70 games in each of the past five seasons.
